APPLICATION DETAILS.Scientific Abstract:
Bowel cancer kills around 16,800 people each year in the UK. Although
many patients receive the same chemotherapy, their outcomes vary
widely. Some people benefit for a long time, while others relapse quickly.
This is because tumours contain groups of cells that respond differently
to treatment and change over time. Better prediction tools could help
APPLICATION DETAILS.Raw Content:
I will develop freely installable software, provide Zenodo DOI archiving
(FAIR principles) and publish open-access in journals e.g. npj Precision
Oncology. I will submit findings to pharmacometrics and clinical
oncology international meetings (e.g. PAGE, ESMO) and disseminate
results through the Manchester Cancer Research Centre (tripartite with
The Christie) and the S:CORT consortium, extending reach across UK
CRC research. I will co-develop plain-language summaries with The
Christie's Patient-Centred Research Group and S:CORT patient
representatives, and engage professional networks (BOPA) to advance
pharmacist-led precision oncology.
